path = "/mnt/data/file-PXHeLULM2fKr3Xxg5fVkpc-text_to_analyse.txt"
with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    text = f.read()
len(text), text[:1000]

text = open("/mnt/data/file-PXHeLULM2fKr3Xxg5fVkpc-text_to_analyse.txt",'r',encoding='utf-8').read()

# Let's print around the first ayah area to inspect characters in detail
start = text.find("﴿")
print(start, text[start-50:start+200])
# Let's extract the exact substring between the decorative brackets, but there is also a quote " included
segment = text[start:start+150]
print(repr(segment))

# find the first ayah inside the decorative bracket pair
open_decor = text.find("﴿")
close_decor = text.find("﴾", open_decor+1)
open_quote = text.find('"', open_decor, close_decor)
# If there is an Arabic quote maybe also check arabic quotes; but shown as standard double quote.
open_quote, open_decor, close_decor, text[open_quote+1:close_decor]

# find hadith chevrons
hadith_open = text.find("«")
hadith_close = text.find("»", hadith_open+1)
hadith_text = text[hadith_open+1:hadith_close]
hadith_open, hadith_close, hadith_text

# find all occurrences of decorative bracket pairs and extract content inside (excluding bracket and not including quotes)
indices = []
pos = 0
while True:
    od = text.find("﴿", pos)
    if od == -1:
        break
    cd = text.find("﴾", od+1)
    if cd == -1:
        break
    # exclude leading quotes if present
    content_start = od+1
    if text[content_start] in ['"', '«', '“', '\"', '']:
        # check for double quote or chevron
        content_start += 1
    # Also if there's leading spaces? unlikely.
    content = text[content_start:cd]
    indices.append((content_start, cd, content))
    pos = cd+1

indices

# find the start of [3] section
start3 = text.find("[3]")
print(start3)
print(text[start3:start3+1200])

# Extract all parentheses contents in [3] segment
seg3 = text[start3:]
pairs = []
stack = []
for i,ch in enumerate(seg3):
    if ch == '(':
        stack.append(i)
    elif ch == ')':
        if stack:
            start_i = stack.pop()
            pairs.append((start_i, i, seg3[start_i+1:i]))
# sort by start index
pairs = sorted(pairs, key=lambda x: x[0])
len(pairs), pairs[:20]

# Build a list of substrings we plan to capture and find their indices in text

targets = []

# 1 main ayah first occurrence
targets.append(("Aya", "ولقد علمتم الذين اعتدوا منكم في السبت فقلنا لهم كونوا قردة خاسئين"))

# 2 hadith
targets.append(("Hadeeth", "لا ترتكبوا ما ارتكبت اليهود، وتستحلوا محارم الله بأدنى الحيل"))

# 3 [1] ayah again with tanween
targets.append(("Aya", "ولقد علمتم الذين اعتدوا منكم في السبت فقلنا لهم كونوا قردةً خاسئين"))

# 4 [1] ayah "لا تقتلوا الصيد وأنتم حرم"
targets.append(("Aya", "لا تقتلوا الصيد وأنتم حرم"))

# 5 [3] "يا أيها الذين امنوا" with diacritics as present; let's copy from the segment content
targets.append(("Aya", "ياأيها الذين آمَنُواْ"))

# 6 [3] "لَيَبْلُوَنَّكُمُ"
targets.append(("Aya", "لَيَبْلُوَنَّكُمُ"))

# 7 [3] "مِّنَ الصيد"
targets.append(("Aya", "مِّنَ الصيد"))

# 8 [3] "لَيَبْلُوَنَّكُمُ الله"
targets.append(("Aya", "لَيَبْلُوَنَّكُمُ الله"))

# 9 [3] "بشيء"
targets.append(("Aya", "بشيء"))

# 10 [3] "بِشَيْءٍ مِّنَ الصيد تَنَالُهُ أَيْدِيكُمْ وَرِمَاحُكُمْ"
targets.append(("Aya", "بِشَيْءٍ مِّنَ الصيد تَنَالُهُ أَيْدِيكُمْ وَرِمَاحُكُمْ"))

# 11 [3] "من" alone parentheses - but we need not annotate all "من"? We'll include this as it's explicit quoted; But to avoid false positives for other 'من's in text, we need to make sure to find the exact instance 
# But searching for "من" will return many. We'll locate the one inside parentheses near there. We'll handle via parent index instead rather than general search. For now we skip general search for "من".

# Now find positions for 1..10
results = []
for ctype, phrase in targets[:-1]:  # exclude the last "من" to handle separately later
    idx = text.find(phrase)
    results.append((ctype, phrase, idx))
results

parentheses_pairs = []
seg3 = text[start3:]
pairs = []
stack = []
for i,ch in enumerate(seg3):
    if ch == '(':
        stack.append(i)
    elif ch == ')':
        if stack:
            s = stack.pop()
            pairs.append((s, i, seg3[s+1:i]))
pairs = sorted(pairs, key=lambda x: x[0])
# compute absolute positions
pairs_abs = [(start3 + s, start3 + e, content) for (s, e, content) in pairs]
pairs_abs

import re

text = open("/mnt/data/file-PXHeLULM2fKr3Xxg5fVkpc-text_to_analyse.txt",'r',encoding='utf-8').read()

start3 = text.find("[3]")
seg3 = text[start3:]

# Build list of punctuation to trim
punct_to_trim = set([' ', '\t', '\n', '\r', '\u200f', '\u200e', '.', '…', '،', ',', '؛', ':', '!', '؟', '«', '»', '“', '”', '(', ')', '[', ']', '﴿', '﴾', '"', "'", 'ـ'])

def trim_edges_and_get_indices(abs_start, abs_end):
    # abs_start points to the '(' char; ans_end points to ')'; we want to trim the content inside
    # actual content range is [abs_start+1, abs_end-1]
    start = abs_start + 1
    end = abs_end  # exclusive index for 'end' of parentheses; content end is end-1
    # Trim leading spaces/punct
    while start < end and text[start] in punct_to_trim:
        start += 1
    # Trim trailing spaces/punct
    while end-1 >= start and text[end-1] in punct_to_trim:
        end -= 1
    # Additional: remove trailing " . . . " pattern for the first parentheses if present
    # The phrase " . . . " may remain trailing within the trimmed substring; we need to trim if trailing punctuation including '.' and spaces remain.
    # We'll loop to remove trailing punctuation '.' and spaces. But we already remove '.' and spaces; But the characters are part of trimmed string if we didn't remove because of extra characters like spaces; Already covered.
    # So return start, end
    return start, end

# Get list of parentheses spans trimmed within [3]
pairs_abs = []
stack = []
for i,ch in enumerate(text):
    if ch == '(':
        stack.append(i)
    elif ch == ')':
        if stack:
            s = stack.pop()
            # we only want those parentheses that are in/after the [3] section? But we can filter by s >= start3
            if s >= start3:
                pairs_abs.append((s, i))

pairs_abs_sorted = sorted(pairs_abs, key=lambda x:x[0])
# For inspection let's map to content
paren_spans = []
for s,e in pairs_abs_sorted:
    content = text[s+1:e]
    paren_spans.append((s,e,content))

# Let's filter those whose content includes Arabic letters and seems to be Qur'an quotes as we saw above
# We'll print them to confirm they match the 8 we saw earlier
cands = []
for (s,e,content) in paren_spans:
    if 'آمَنُوا' in content or 'لَيَبْلُوَنَّكُمُ' in content or 'مِّنَ الصيد' in content or 'الله' in content or content.strip() == 'بشيء' or 'بِشَيْءٍ' in content or content.strip() == 'من':
        ts, te = trim_edges_and_get_indices(s,e)
        cands.append((s,e,ts,te,text[ts:te]))

cands

# Verify each candidate substring within text to ensure indices yield correct text

spans = []

# 1 Ayah initial
spans.append(("Aya", 186, 251))

# 2 hadith
spans.append(("Hadeeth", 562, 622))

# 3 [1] ayah repeated with tanween
spans.append(("Aya", 1124, 1190))

# 4 [1] ayah "لا تقتلوا"
spans.append(("Aya", 1239, 1264))

# 5 [3] parentheses trimmed list items
spans.append(("Aya", 2850, 2871))

spans.append(("Aya", 3130, 3147))

spans.append(("Aya", 3235, 3246))

spans.append(("Aya", 3460, 3482))

spans.append(("Aya", 3720, 3724))

spans.append(("Aya", 4300, 4356))

spans.append(("Aya", 4378, 4380))

spans.append(("Aya", 4393, 4404))

for ctype, s, e in spans:
    substring = text[s:e]
    print(ctype, s, e, repr(substring))
